assertion.text: Erdős Problem #1095: declared status 'open'. Formalized: yes. Let $g(k)>k+1$ be the smallest $n$ such that all prime factors of $\binom{n}{k}$ are $>k$. Estimate $g(k)$. Current best: In \cite{EES74} they further conjecture that\[\limsup \frac{g(k+1)}{g(k)}=\infty\]and\[\liminf \frac{g(k+1)}{g(k)}=0.\]The lower bound was improved by Erd\H{o}s, Lacampagne, and Selfridge \cite{ELS93} and Granville and Ramar\'{e} \cite{GrRa96}. The current record is\[g(k) \gg \exp(c(\log k)^2)\]for some $c>0$, due to Konyagin \cite{Ko99b}. Prize: no. OEIS: A003458. Tags: binomial coefficients, number theory.
